title: Supplier Delivery Performance
spl:
index=supply sourcetype="edi:asn"
| eval on_time=if(actual_arrival <= promised_date,1,0)
| stats sum(on_time) as ot, count as total, avg((actual_arrival-promised_date)/86400) as avg_late_days by supplier_id
| eval otif_pct=round(100*ot/total,1)
| sort otif_pct
| head 20
description: On-time delivery % and lead-time variance from ASN/OTIF feeds support supplier scorecards and production planning.
value: On-time delivery % and lead-time variance from ASN/OTIF feeds support supplier scorecards and production planning.
implementation: Join to GRN for quantity accuracy; exclude force majeure with reason codes.
visualization: Bar chart (OTIF % by supplier), Table (worst performers), Trend (rolling 13 weeks).
knownFalsePositives: Similar activity during production startups, control downloads, and approved remote maintenance—triage with the SCADA HMI and change log.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: TMS, EDI 856/855, MES receipts.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="edi:asn"`, `sourcetype="wms:receipt"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Join to GRN for quantity accuracy; exclude force majeure with reason codes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=supply sourcetype="edi:asn"
| eval on_time=if(actual_arrival <= promised_date,1,0)
| stats sum(on_time) as ot, count as total, avg((actual_arrival-promised_date)/86400) as avg_late_days by supplier_id
| eval otif_pct=round(100*ot/total,1)
| sort otif_pct
| head 20
```

Understanding this SPL

**Supplier Delivery Performance** — On-time delivery % and lead-time variance from ASN/OTIF feeds support supplier scorecards and production planning.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="edi:asn"`, `sourcetype="wms:receipt"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): TMS, EDI 856/855, MES receipts. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: supply; **sourcetype**: edi:asn. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=supply, sourcetype="edi:asn". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **on_time** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by supplier_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **otif_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Limits the number of rows with `head`.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search when the process is in a known state. Compare key fields and alarm states to the SCADA or DCS HMI and, where relevant, the engineering tool for the PLC. Confirm sourcetypes and field extractions match the vendor feed.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (OTIF % by supplier), Table (worst performers), Trend (rolling 13 weeks).
grandmaExplanation: We watch your plant control systems and field data. We help you know if something is off with this area: supplier delivery performance before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
